div1.entryFree: Well-a-day, exclamation expressive of dislike or grief, == ah, woe, alas: O w., Mistress Ford! having an honest man to your husband, to give him such cause of suspicion, Wiv. III, 3, 106. I am as well in my wits as any man in Illyria. W. that you were, sir, Tw. IV, 2, 116. O w., lady, if he be not drawn now, H5 II, 1, 38. Ah, w., he's dead, Rom. III, 2, 37. O w., that ever I was born, IV, 5, 15. when, w., we could scarcely help ourselves, Per. II, 1, 23. Substantively: his daughter's woe and heavy w. in her unholy service, Per. IV, 4, 49.
